task_completion.description: Divide the space using at least 3 new partition walls and install at least 3 doors/windows, including an observation window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan or 3D view shows at least 3 interior partition walls added to divide the space. There are at least 3 doors or windows present, with at least one window clearly positioned as an observation window between the taproom and the brewing area.

task_completion.description: Define at least 4 rooms and apply distinct floor colors/textures to at least 3 of them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The floor plan shows at least 4 distinct room areas defined (often visible by room labels or boundaries). The 3D view or 2D plan clearly displays at least 3 different floor colors or textures applied to these rooms to visually differentiate the zones.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Bar zone and Communal seating area with the required tables, counters, and chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Bar area containing at least 3 counter/table surfaces and 8 bar stools/chairs. Look for a Communal seating hall with at least 4 large tables and 16 chairs. Overall, verify there are at least 7 tables/counters and 24 chairs/stools visible in the design.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Lounge and Merchandise corner with the required seating, tables, and display units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Lounge zone containing at least 3 sofas or armchairs and 2 low tables. Look for a Merchandise/retail corner containing at least 5 shelving or display units.

task_completion.description: Decorate with lighting and plants, and ensure the design contains at least 50 furniture items in total.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 6 lighting fixtures and 4 plants placed throughout the taproom. Check the software's furniture list panel (if visible) or visually assess the density of the floor plan to confirm it contains at least 50 total items.
The application title bar shows the project name without an 'unsaved changes' indicator (such as an asterisk), or a save dialog/confirmation is visible in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the design software to create the floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the design software's GUI (e.g., dragging furniture, drawing walls, using dialog boxes) rather than running terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The floor plan was built progressively rather than loading a pre-made completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the floor plan evolving step-by-step (e.g., empty space -> walls -> rooms -> furniture) across multiple screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the design software by editing the raw project file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors or IDEs are open showing the raw XML/JSON of the project file being manually modified.
